vocabulary.chinese: 難忘的　good or special and thus worth remembering; unforgettable
vocabulary.example_en: The amazing white sand beaches, friendly people, and delicious food made our trip to Thailand truly memorable.
Nancy is good at memorizing texts within a very short period of time, but she usually forgets them after a few days.
Laney’s visit to her grandparents’ home in Chiayi brought back childhood memories.
Fiona has a really good memory. She can remember most of her best friends’ phone numbers.

vocabulary.chinese: [C, U] 回答；回覆　something that is said, written, or done as an answer
vocabulary.example_en: The question was really hard, so Dylan thought for several seconds before he made a reply to the teacher.
Brad has the habit of replying to every email right after he reads it.

vocabulary.chinese: 大喊；大吼　to shout loudly or angrily
vocabulary.example_en: One neighbor called the police because George was yelling at his family in the middle of the night.

vocabulary.chinese: 完美的　with no mistakes or problems
vocabulary.example_en: John was very careful with his car, so it was still in perfect condition after five years of use.

vocabulary.chinese: 意外地；不小心地　by chance or by mistake
vocabulary.example_en: Sheila accidentally spilled coffee on her new dress, and it left a mark.
The horrible car accident took the lives of several children.
An accidental wrong turn led us to a beautiful, undiscovered beach.

vocabulary.chinese: [C, U] 失敗　the fact that someone or something does not achieve success
vocabulary.example_en: The meeting was a total failure since nothing was decided at all.
Yvonne’s grandmother recalled that she married the most handsome man in town at twenty.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 報告；介紹　a meeting where something is shown to a group of people by one or multiple speakers
vocabulary.example_en: Each student must give a presentation on one movie at the end of the semester.
Hannah is going to present her report to her professor tomorrow.
Michelle presented her best friend with flowers at her birthday party.

vocabulary.chinese: 故意不參加；不做（該做的事）　 (skipped—skipped—skipping) to not do something that you normally do or are expected to do
vocabulary.example_en: I don’t usually skip class because I don’t like to fall behind my classmates.
James was running out of time, so he skipped the last part of his talk.

vocabulary.chinese: 破壞；毀壞　to spoil or destroy something entirely
vocabulary.example_en: Mandy’s sandcastle was suddenly ruined when a big wave splashed onto it.
Many temples, churches, and libraries in China fell into ruin during the period between 1966 and 1976.

vocabulary.chinese: 尷尬的；難堪的　feeling ashamed or uncomfortable in public because of something that has happened or been said
vocabulary.example_en: Amy was really embarrassed about her mistake during the dance class, though nobody noticed it.

vocabulary.chinese: 令人尷尬的；令人難堪的
vocabulary.example_en: Tina experienced an embarrassing situation when she fell asleep during the yoga class and the teacher had to wake her up.
Did I embarrass you when I told our friends that you and your classmate Ken used to be dating?
To Dave’s embarrassment, he found that he was wearing his T-shirt inside out.

vocabulary.chinese: 沮喪的；消沉的　feeling extremely sad
vocabulary.example_en: Emilia felt deeply depressed after her smartphone was stolen.
The sudden death of her pet goldfish really depressed Cindy and her brother Tim.
After a long period of deep depression, Amanda finally found happiness again.

vocabulary.chinese: 使難過；使沮喪　(upset—upset—upsetting) to make someone unhappy or disappointed
vocabulary.example_en: You shouldn’t speak to your parents in an impolite way since you could upset them.
Kyle was deeply upset about not winning first place in the swimming race.

vocabulary.chinese: 認真思考；沉思　to think carefully about something that has happened
vocabulary.example_en: Barbara spent a whole week reflecting on her college choice.
The lake was calm and reflected Tom’s face when he looked down at the water.
The funny little dog started barking wildly at its own reflection in the mirror.

vocabulary.chinese: 提醒；使想起　to make someone remember to do something or to make someone remember something or someone
vocabulary.example_en: Ms. Chen reminded her students of the importance of checking their homework.

vocabulary.chinese: 了解；領悟　to clearly understand something

vocabulary.example_en: The young woman finally realized her dream of visiting every country in the world by herself.

vocabulary.chinese: 傳統的　following or belonging to the long-held customs and ways of life of a group of people, unchanged for many years
vocabulary.example_en: Bella lived in Mexico for several years and can cook several traditional Mexican dishes.
The tradition of giving red envelopes to children on Lunar New Year’s Eve started thousands of years ago in China.
Fortunately, few people were hurt when the building fell down during the earthquake.
We are fortunate to have clean drinking water in most homes in our country.

grammar.explanation: 意為「一些些……」，後面接複數可數名詞。　 同 some

examples.en: A few students raised their hands when the teacher asked a question.
examples.ch: 幾個學生在老師提問時舉起了手。

grammar.explanation: 意為「第一次」。

examples.en: I went diving for the first time last summer with my friends. We had so much fun!
examples.ch: 我去年夏天和朋友們第一次去潛水。我們玩得超開心！

grammar.explanation: 意為「像是……」，用於舉例。

examples.en: We use tools such as email and messaging apps to stay in touch with friends and family.
examples.ch: 我們使用(像是電子郵件、即時通訊應用程式等)工具來和朋友及家人保持聯繫。

grammar.explanation: 意為「對……大吼大叫」。

examples.en: The coach yelled at the players for not trying hard enough during the game.
examples.ch: 教練因球員們在比賽中不夠努力而對他們大吼大叫。

grammar.explanation: 意為「其餘的……；剩下的……」。=the others

examples.en: While the teacher talked to one student, the rest of the class waited patiently.
examples.ch: 當老師在和一個學生說話時，其餘的同學耐心地等待。

grammar.explanation: 意為「打開……」。

examples.en: I accidentally turned on the wrong stove burner when I was cooking. Fortunately, no one got hurt!
examples.ch: 我下廚的時候，不小心打開了錯誤的爐灶開關。幸好沒有人受傷。

grammar.explanation: 此為感官動詞的用法，意為「看／聞／嚐／聽／感覺起來像……」。  連綴V + adj.

examples.en: The meat tasted like rotten food, so I spit it out immediately. The meat tasted bad/badly, so I spit it out immediately.
examples.ch: 那塊肉吃起來像腐壞的食物，所以我馬上就吐出來了。

grammar.explanation: 轉承詞，意為「然而……」。

examples.en: Anna really liked the T-shirt displayed in the store. However , she didn’t have enough money to buy it.
examples.ch: Anna非常喜歡店裡展示的T恤。然而，她沒有足夠的錢可以買。

grammar.explanation: 意為「認真看待……」。

examples.en: Though his parents had warned him, Tom did not take his summer reading assignment seriously .
examples.ch: 儘管父母已經警告過他，Tom並沒有認真看待他的暑期閱讀作業。

grammar.formula: remind sb. to VR/that S + V
grammar.explanation: 意為「提醒某人做……」，另remind sb. of N 為「使某人想起……」。

examples.en: The local government set up a sign to remind people to conserve water during the drought.
examples.ch: 當地政府設立了一個標誌，提醒人們在乾旱期間節約用水。

examples.en: The scent of fresh bread baking reminds me of visiting my grandmother when I was young.
examples.ch: 新鮮出爐的麵包的香味讓我想起小時候去探望祖母的情景。

grammar.explanation: 意為「確認……」。

examples.en: I always make sure that the door is locked before I leave the house.
examples.ch: 在我離開房子前，我總是確保門鎖上了。

grammar.explanation: 意為「因為……」 同 because。since亦可表「自從……」，此時所帶的子句要用過去式/過去時間點，主要子句則用現在完成式。

examples.en: I decided to stay home since I was not feeling well that morning.
examples.ch: 因為那天早上我感到不舒服，所以我決定待在家裡。

examples.en: I have been fascinated with art since I took an art history class last year.
examples.ch: 自去年選修了藝術史課程以來，我一直對藝術著迷。

grammar.explanation: 意為「穿上……（服裝）」。

examples.en: Every Halloween, my son likes to dress up in a firefighter costume.
examples.ch: 每年萬聖夜時，我兒子喜歡穿上消防員服裝。

grammar.explanation: 連接詞，意為「當……的時候」。  同 when也可作為「雖然」。  同 although

examples.en: While Mike was studying, his sister was playing loud music in her room.
examples.ch: 當Mike在讀書的時候，他妹妹在她房間播放嘈雜音樂。

examples.en: While I enjoy teaching, grading tests is often tedious.
examples.ch: 雖然我喜歡教學，但改考卷往往乏味。

grammar.explanation: 「在……要結束時」

examples.en: At the end of the speech, Emily put her stuff in her bag and was ready to leave the classroom.
examples.ch: 演講快結束時，Emily開始收拾背包並準備離開教室。

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 戀愛關係　a close or loving friendship between two people
vocabulary.example_en: Marc and Amy have been in a relationship since their first date four years ago.
Maggie has a very close relationship with her grandparents because she lived with them for most of her childhood.
In relation to your question in the email, I still don’t know the answer.
Studies have related smoking to many health problems, so you shouldn’t take up this habit.

vocabulary.chinese: 令人滿意的　 bringing pleasure to a person by providing something that person wants or needs
vocabulary.example_en: Billy thinks it is satisfying to drink a big cup of iced tea after he exercises.
The hotpot dinner really satisfied all of the hungry guests.
Jessica smiled with satisfaction when she saw 100 on her test sheet.

vocabulary.chinese: 激勵　to cause someone to have the desire, confidence, or enthusiasm to do a good thing or improve himself or herself
vocabulary.example_en: The superheroine Wonder Woman has inspired a lot of girls with her bravery and strength.
Carly was inspired by one of the paintings in the museum and decided to take an art class.
Paulina draws inspiration from her relationships with others when she writes poetry.

vocabulary.chinese: 與他人建立良好關係　to develop a relationship with someone
vocabulary.example_en: The two friends connected with each other when they rode their bikes around the country together.
Jason connected his smartphone to the TV, so he could show his friends some pictures on the bigger screen.
The teacher failed to see the connection between Bonnie’s story and her being late for class.

vocabulary.chinese: 溝通　to share information, facts, ideas, or feelings with others
vocabulary.example_en: The Internet allows us to communicate quickly and easily with people all over the world.
Telephone calls have become a less common means of communication as many people now prefer apps like LINE.

vocabulary.chinese: 使某人初次體驗　to inform or teach someone about something that he or she doesn’t know much about
vocabulary.example_en: This two-minute video introduces the public to the latest iPhone and its new features.
Mr. Cheng introduced Ms. Lee to his wife as his hardest-working employee.
The waiter gave the guests a detailed introduction to that day’s menu.

vocabulary.chinese: 自始至終　occurring during an entire period of time
vocabulary.example_en: In some countries, like Singapore, the weather stays hot throughout the year.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] （電郵或手機等的）訊息　a piece of writing such as an email or SMS (short message service) sent to someone electronically
vocabulary.example_en: Kylie sent a text message to her boss to tell him that she would be ten minutes late for work.

vocabulary.chinese: vi. 刷新　to click on something to make the latest information appear
vocabulary.example_en: Olivia refreshed her Facebook Feed to check for new posts from her friends.
Carl refreshed himself with a large glass of ice-cold water.

vocabulary.chinese: 浪漫的；愛情的　related to love or a couple’s personal relationship
vocabulary.example_en: Nicole and Sam were just friends for many years and never developed a romantic relationship.
Cindy is glad that her love life is much simpler than most romance novels.

vocabulary.chinese: vt. 專注；專心　to pay particular attention to one thing, situation, or person
vocabulary.example_en: Jason couldn’t focus on the action in the movie because he was trying to read the words on the screen.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] usually sing. 關注的人或事物
vocabulary.example_en: The main focus for today’s history class will be World Wars I and II.

vocabulary.chinese: 忽略　to not pay attention to something or someone
vocabulary.example_en: Grace totally ignored her younger brother as he made silly faces at her.
Out of ignorance, the students made jokes about the exchange student’s country.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 注意力　the act of focusing on something or someone
vocabulary.example_en: The article draws our attention to the importance of being good parents in today’s world.

vocabulary.chinese: 否認　(denied—denied—denying) to refuse to admit that something is true
vocabulary.example_en: Mr. Lin denied giving a box of candy to his daughter, but Mrs. Lin didn’t believe him.

vocabulary.chinese: vt. 回應　to give a spoken or written reply to someone or something
vocabulary.example_en: Chris responded to his mom’s Facebook message with a smiling cat sticker.
The students in the class made no response to the teacher’s difficult question.

vocabulary.chinese: 感到失望的　sad or upset because something you hoped for has not happened or has not occurred in the way that you expected
vocabulary.example_en: Fiona was really disappointed that her favorite character died at the end of the movie.
It really disappointed Sally that her friend Lindsay never replied to her message.
To Amy’s great disappointment, her parents were late for the dance show and missed her part.

vocabulary.chinese: 針對某人地　in a way that is regarded as hurtful to one’s self
vocabulary.example_en: Please don’t take my comment personally. I’m complaining about all the drivers in our country, not just you.
Mrs. Thompson has a lot of personal experience working with kids who can’t focus in class.

grammar.explanation: 表「我將離開你」之義，是用現在進行式代替未來式，常用於「來去動詞」，如come、go、leave、visit、arrive等，表「即將」之義。

examples.en: Sarah is going to a concert held in Taipei.
examples.ch: Sarah明天要去參加一場在臺北舉辦的演唱會。

grammar.explanation: 為現在完成式的句型，表過去某時持續到現在為止的動作或狀態，或到目前為止的經驗。

examples.en: Carter has lived in Keelung for ten years, so he is used to rainy days.
examples.ch: Carter已經住在基隆十年了，所以習慣下雨的日子。

grammar.explanation: 為形容詞，I’m afraid (that) S + V在此為「恐怕⋯⋯」，用以委婉告知會讓某人不開心、不舒服或失望的事。另外afraid有「害怕的」之義，義同scared，後面可加of + N/V-ing或to + VR。

examples.en: I’m afraid that your bank account was hacked, and all of the money in it was withdrawn.
examples.ch: 你的銀行帳戶恐怕已經被駭客入侵，裡面所有的錢都被領走了。

examples.en: Tiffany is afraid/scared of sleeping alone at night because she is afraid/scared of ghosts.
examples.ch: Tiffany晚上不敢一個人睡覺，因為她怕鬼。

grammar.formula: Leaving you really breaks my heart...
grammar.explanation: 此句話Leaving you為動名詞作主詞用，視為單一事件，後面接單數動詞，因此動詞須用第三人稱單數，也可改為虛主詞It開頭的寫法：It really breaks my heart to leave you.

examples.en: Writing such a long article usually takes Asher at least two hours.
examples.ch: 寫一篇這麼長的文章通常要花Asher至少兩個小時。

grammar.explanation: 表「某事物使某人心碎」之義，並衍生出heartbreaking（令人心碎的）和heartbroken（感到心碎的）兩形容詞，前者多用於修飾事物，後者則用於描述人的感覺。

examples.en: Seeing Grandma seriously ill and lying in bed all day really breaks my heart . It really breaks my heart to see Grandma seriously ill and lying in bed all day.
examples.ch: 看著奶奶重病且整天躺在病床上的樣子讓我心都碎了。

grammar.explanation: 度過艱難的時候timehave a good time 意為「玩得很愉快」。  同 enjoy oneself

examples.en: We all had a great/good time at the party tonight. =We all enjoyed ourselves at the party tonight.
examples.ch: 我們在今晚的聚會都玩得很開心，感謝你邀請我們。

grammar.explanation: 在此為過去分詞作形容詞用，表「感到無聊的」，用來描述人的感覺，若是boring則為「令人無聊的」。

examples.en: During summer vacation, I feel bored at home with nothing to do.
examples.ch: 我暑假在家好無聊，沒什麼事情可以做。

examples.en: The movie was so boring that Tony fell asleep halfway through it.
examples.ch: 這部電影太無聊了，Tony看一半就睡著了。

grammar.explanation: 人+ N為「問某人某事」之義，N可以是名詞片語或if/wh-子句。

examples.en: Luke kept asking me questions about my private life, which made me very uncomfortable.
examples.ch: Luke一直問我私人生活的問題，這讓我很不舒服。

examples.en: Harper asked her mom if/whether she could attend a summer camp with her elder sister.
examples.ch: Harper問媽媽她是否能跟姐姐一起參加夏令營。

grammar.explanation: 為「幫助某人做某事」，也可寫成help + sb. + to VR，to可省略。另外也有 help + sb. + with + N 的用法。

examples.en: Judy always helps her mom with the housework after school.
examples.ch: Judy放學後總是協助媽媽做家事。

examples.en: I helped my friend move his stuff to his new dormitory yesterday.
examples.ch: 昨天我幫朋友把他的東西搬到新的宿舍。

grammar.explanation: 表「自從」，即「從過去某時到現在為止」，主要子句多用現在完成式，可加名詞或子句，常與ever連用。

examples.en: I’ve wanted to visit Niagara Falls since I saw photos of it in a geography textbook.
examples.ch: 自從我在地理教科書上看到尼加拉大瀑布的照片，我就一直想去。

examples.en: Evelyn has been having nightmares since last week , but she doesn’t know the reason.
examples.ch: Evelyn從上週開始就一直做惡夢，但她不知道原因是什麼。

grammar.explanation: 為「（使）某人醒來」，wake可作為及物或不及物動詞，三態為wake-woke-woken。

examples.en: After going to sleep feeling stressed last night, I woke up to the sound of birds chirping this morning.
examples.ch: 昨晚備感壓力入睡後，今天早上我在鳥兒的鳴叫聲中醒來。

grammar.explanation: 為副詞片語，表「一再地」之義，亦可用over and over again 或 time and (time) again。

examples.en: Jackson loves the movie Spider-Man: Homecoming so much that he has watched it again and again .
examples.ch: Jackson非常喜歡《蜘蛛人：返校日》這部電影，他反覆看了一次又一次。

grammar.explanation: 在此為「聽起來」之義，後面可接形容詞、like + N或as if + 子句，屬連綴動詞的用法，同樣屬感官類的連綴動詞還有look、taste、smell、feel等。

examples.en: What Julian just proposed sounds like a good idea.
examples.ch: Julian剛剛的建議聽起來是個好主意。

examples.en: When Eva announced the rules, she sounded as if she was the class leader. 假設語氣
examples.ch: Eva在宣布規則的時候，聽起來彷彿她是班長。

grammar.explanation: 比較級（+ and比較級）表「愈來愈……」，用進行式表示逐漸改變的狀態。  同 become

examples.en: I am getting nervous as the time for my speech is approaching.
examples.ch: 當我演說的時間接近時，我變得愈來愈緊張。

grammar.explanation: 為副詞，表「其他」，與不定代名詞（如something、someone等）或wh-疑問詞連用。

examples.en: The waiter recommended pizza, but I decided to try something else from the menu.
examples.ch: 服務生推薦了披薩，但我決定嘗試菜單上的其它東西。

grammar.explanation: 表「忙於做某事」。

examples.en: Donna is busy planning a ten-day trip to Italy.
examples.ch: Donna正在忙著規劃到義大利的十天之旅。

examples.en: Steve has been busy with/doing a math assignment recently, so he has stayed up late several times.
examples.ch: Steve最近忙著做一份數學作業，所以他熬夜了好幾次。

grammar.explanation: 表「停止手邊正在做的事」，stop to + VR 表「停下原本的動作去做某事」。

examples.en: As soon as the phone rang, Kyle stopped cleaning the floor to answer it.
examples.ch: 電話一響，Kyle就停止清理地板去接電話。

examples.en: After studying for three hours in a row, Thomas stopped to
examples.ch: 連續讀了三個小時的書之後，Thomas停下來休息了一下。

grammar.explanation: 在此為「意味著、有某種結果」之義，後面可接名詞、動名詞或that子句。

examples.en: Having a baby means giving up all your free time. You’d better think about the decision carefully!
examples.ch: 生了小孩意味著放棄所有的自由時間。你最好仔細考慮一下這個決定！

examples.en: If you want to catch the first train tomorrow, it means we’ll have to leave the house before 6 a.m.
examples.ch: 如果你想趕上明天第一班火車，這意味我們必須在早上六點前離開家。

grammar.explanation: 表「⋯⋯的方法」

examples.en: It’s said that doing yoga is a good way to relieve stress.
examples.ch: 據說做瑜珈是紓解壓力的好方法。

grammar.explanation: 為「花費（時間／金錢）」之義，用法為人 + spend + 時間/金錢 + on + N/V-ing。

examples.en: Pat goes to Europe three times a year; that is, he spends most of his money on vacations// taking a trip.
examples.ch: Pat一年去歐洲三次；也就是說，他把大部分的錢都花在度假了。

examples.en: As difficult as it is, many career women still hope to balance work and family life.
examples.ch: 雖然很難，許多職業婦女還是希望能兼顧工作和家庭生活。

grammar.explanation: 表「最後一次」，同理，「for the first time」表「第一次」

examples.en: The foreign tourist tried stinky tofu for the first time , and he loved the unique flavor.
examples.ch: 這位外國遊客第一次嘗試臭豆腐，他很喜歡這種獨特的味道。

3.title: Lesson 3Do Animals Sleep like You and Me?

vocabulary.chinese: vi. 想知道　to consider something and try to decide what is true, what will happen, what one should do, and so on
vocabulary.example_en: Samuel wonders if he will lose some weight after he joins the swimming club.

vocabulary.chinese: 令人大感驚奇的　very surprising, incredible, and hard to believe
vocabulary.example_en: It’s amazing how bright the stars are outside of the city at night!
It often amazes people that pigs are smarter than dogs.
The travelers stood there in amazement as they looked at the huge rainbow up in the sky.

vocabulary.chinese: [U, C] 平均　an amount, type, or level that is in the middle
vocabulary.example_en: Helen usually scores above average on her exams because she always studies much harder than her classmates.
The runners finished the race in an average of two minutes and ten seconds.
Even though the price of some new smartphones is high, the average price continues to drop every year.
The average teenager would rather send a LINE message than write a letter to a friend.

vocabulary.chinese: 因此　as a result of something that was just mentioned
vocabulary.example_en: The boots were much more expensive than Tina had expected; therefore, she didn’t buy them.

vocabulary.chinese: 小睡　(napped—napped—napping) to sleep for a short period of time during the day
vocabulary.example_en: Craig has a bad habit of staying up late and then napping several times during the day.
Everybody in the Su family likes to take a nap right after lunch on weekends.

vocabulary.chinese: 漂浮　to remain on or near the surface of the water or of another liquid and not sink
vocabulary.example_en: Jenna threw a piece of bread into the pond and watched it float until a fish ate it.
The couple lay on the grass and enjoyed the view of the clouds as they floated across the sky.

vocabulary.chinese: 避免　to prevent something, usually bad, from happening
vocabulary.example_en: The family avoided getting stuck in the traffic jam by leaving home very early in the morning.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] （水、地板等）表面　the top layer of water or land, or the outer layer of something
vocabulary.example_en: When too many people boarded the little boat, it began to sink below the surface of the water.

vocabulary.chinese: vt. 呼吸　to take air into your lungs and blow it out through your nose or mouth
vocabulary.example_en: The yoga teacher told her students to breathe in as they lifted their arms above their heads.
The little boy was out of breath after his father chased him around the park.

vocabulary.chinese: 醒著的　not sleeping, no longer sleeping, or not yet asleep
vocabulary.example_en: The loud music from the neighbors’ party kept the whole family awake all night.
Sherry awoke from a deep sleep and saw that her dog was sitting on the bed and watching her.

vocabulary.chinese: 令人印象深刻的　able to make one feel amazed because something or someone is very good, skillful, etc.
vocabulary.example_en: It’s so impressive that Brian can say “hello” and “thank you” in more than fifteen languages!
Michelle liked to impress her friends with her yo-yo tricks.
The new worker left a good impression on most of the staff members on her first day.

vocabulary.chinese: [C, U] 距離　the length of the space between two places or things
vocabulary.example_en: The distance between the eastern end of Russia and the western one is about ten thousand kilometers.
If you live in a big city, the downtown area may be distant from your home.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 旅行　a trip that is usually long in terms of distance or time
vocabulary.example_en: After high school, the two best friends plan to go on a journey around the world.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 飛行　the condition, state, or process of flying
vocabulary.example_en: Baby birds usually take flight for the first time after they are only about two or three weeks old.
When Mrs. Kuo arrived at the airport, she learned that her flight was going to be thirty minutes late.

vocabulary.chinese: sing. 多種樣式、變化　several different types of something
vocabulary.example_en: That store sells a wide variety of children’s toys and books.
Smartphone cases vary in color, size, shape, and material, but all of them have the same basic function.
The festival was canceled for various reasons, like poor weather and lack of interest.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 生物　a living thing such as an animal, or an imagined being such as a monster
vocabulary.example_en: Nick is kind-hearted. He wouldn’t hurt any living creature, not even a mosquito.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 專家；行家　a person with advanced knowledge, skills, or training in a particular field or subject
vocabulary.example_en: Mr. Yang is an expert in math. I’m glad to have him as my tutor.

vocabulary.chinese: vt. 生存；倖存　to stay alive or continue to exist
vocabulary.example_en: Some animals are able to survive from season to season by changing the color of their fur.
The news report said that the missing mountain climbers had a low chance of survival.

grammar.explanation: 可用來對現在某個事實的可能性表示推測。

examples.en: I may not have all the answers, but I’ll do my best to assist you.
examples.ch: 我可能沒有全部的答案，但我會盡力協助你。

grammar.explanation: 以現在完成式詢問對方是否有做某事的經驗。ever一字為加強語氣用，表示at any time之義。

examples.en: Have you ever tried scuba diving? If not, you have to give it a try!
examples.ch: 你有嘗試過配戴水肺潛水嗎？如果沒有，一定要試試看！

grammar.explanation: 為疑問詞how所引導的間接問句，為名詞子句，作為wondered的受詞。

examples.en: Nobody knew why Mr. Chen was angry . He started to scold us as soon as he walked into the classroom.
examples.ch: 沒有人知道陳老師為什麼生氣。他一走進教室就開始罵我們。

grammar.explanation: 表「……其中的一些」，some在此為代名詞，of後的名詞需以定冠詞the或所有格限定其範圍。

examples.en: Some of the students in the class are going on a field trip tomorrow.
examples.ch: 班上有一些學生明天要去參加戶外教學。

grammar.explanation: 在此為形容詞，表「以下的」、「接下來的」之義，如the following instructions、the following day等。

examples.en: To make sure you know how to operate the machine, please listen to
examples.ch: 為了確認你們知道如何操作這個機器，請仔細聽以下的指示。

examples.en: Ella is an outstanding astronaut. In fact , she once participated in a secret space mission.
examples.ch: Ella是一位傑出的太空人。事實上，她曾經參與一項祕密太空任務。

examples.en: Pinocchio is the main character in an Italian novel. Whenever he lies , his nose grows longer.
examples.ch: 皮諾丘是一本義大利小說中的主角。每當他說謊，他的鼻子就會變長。

examples.en: The box looks very heavy. If you like, you can lay it on the ground.
examples.ch: 那個盒子看起來好重。如果你願意的話，你可以把它放在地上。

grammar.explanation: 為「存在句」，表某人事物的存在，為「有……」之意，動詞的單複數視其後的名詞而定。

examples.en: In the park, there is a sand pit for children to play in, and there are also benches for parents to sit on.
examples.ch: 在公園裡，有一個讓孩子們玩耍的沙坑，還有供父母們坐著的長椅。

grammar.explanation: 最高級 + 複數名詞 = among the + 最高級 + 複數名詞，意為「最⋯⋯之一」。

examples.en: Pandas are one of the cutest animals in the world.
examples.ch: 貓熊是世界上最可愛的動物之一。

grammar.explanation: 在此表「某件事正在進行的同時（另一件事也發生了）」，常與進行式連用。

examples.en: Audrey was listening to a podcast on her phone while her mom was cleaning downstairs.
examples.ch: Audrey的媽媽在樓下打掃時，她正在用手機聽podcast。

grammar.explanation: 為副詞，此處用來修飾比較級，表「更加⋯⋯」，其他可用來修飾比較級的副詞還有far、a lot、even、still、yet、a little等。

examples.en: This villa is much more expensive than an apartment, so Ethan can’t afford it.
examples.ch: 這間別墅比公寓貴多了，所以Ethan負擔不起。

grammar.explanation: 為不定詞片語，放在句首用來表達目的，也可寫為In order to + VR, ....

examples.en: To join the Olympic Games in Paris in 2024, the young athlete practices very hard.
examples.ch: 為了要參加2024年的巴黎奧運，這位年輕的運動員非常努力地練習。

grammar.explanation: 在此處的意思是「一次」。

examples.en: I can’t handle so many problems at a time . Please let me deal with them one by one.
examples.ch: 我無法一次處理這麼多的問題。請讓我一個一個處理。

grammar.explanation: 的用法與have to類似，兩者皆解釋為「必須」，後接原形動詞。但 must本身無過去式，若要表達其過去式，僅能用had to。

examples.en: The case is very urgent. You must deal with it right now.
examples.ch: 這個案子非常緊急。你必須立刻處理。

grammar.explanation: 在此為形容詞，後接名詞，用來指稱 「（兩者之中的）另一個」。此句中因為海豚的腦部分成兩區，所以「剩下來的另一半」就用the other half。

examples.en: Jenny has two daughters. One likes to listen to Western pop music, and the other likes K-pop.
examples.ch: Jenny有兩個女兒。一個喜歡聽西洋流行樂，另一個則是喜歡韓國流行音樂。

grammar.explanation: 為連綴動詞，後接形容詞，表「保持」之意。  同 remain, keep

examples.en: Adam goes to a gym three times a week to stay healthy .
examples.ch: Adam為了保持健康，一週去健身房三次。

grammar.explanation: 解釋為「（未限定的）另外一個」。就本句而言，奇妙的動物睡眠習慣有很多種，此處僅介紹其中一種。

examples.en: The latest earphones from this brand are too expensive, and their terrible sound quality is another problem .
examples.ch: 這個牌子的最新款耳機太貴了，而且糟糕的音質是另一個問題。

examples.en: The best way of achieving your goal is to stay focused.
examples.ch: 達成目標的最佳方式就是保持專注。

examples.en: Since Judy keeps complaining about her job, why doesn’t she just quit and look for a new one?
examples.ch: 既然Judy一直抱怨她的工作，為什麼不乾脆辭職去找一份新的工作呢？

examples.en: I go to work by MRT every day since I can’t afford a car.
examples.ch: 我每天搭捷運上班因為我買不起汽車。

examples.en: After months of training, Mike was finally able to climb the mountain and reach its summit.
examples.ch: 經過了數個月的訓練之後，Mike終於能夠攀登這座山並且登上了山頂。

grammar.explanation: 表「似乎；看起來」，為連綴動詞，後面可加形容詞，亦可加to + VR。

examples.en: The new principal seems very friendly and easy to get along with.
examples.ch: 新來的校長看起來很友善又好相處。

examples.en: Catherine seemed to disagree with the answer, as she was frowning and saying nothing.
examples.ch: Catherine似乎不同意這個回答，因為她皺著眉而且什麼也沒說。

grammar.explanation: 在此為副詞，表「然而」之義，若要連接兩個子句，則與and連用或用分號連接。

examples.en: Kathy practiced the piano for hours every day, and yet she still couldn’t master the difficult piece.
examples.ch: Kathy每天練習鋼琴好幾個小時，然而，她還是無法掌握那首困難的樂曲。

4.title: Lesson 4Sniffing* Out More Than Just Bones

vocabulary.chinese: 敏感的　able to notice very subtle things such as changes or differences that others may not be aware of
vocabulary.example_en: Kelly is very sensitive to flavors. She can taste any food or drink and name it without seeing it.
Jamie is keen on making his classroom’s Facebook page the most interesting one in the school.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 一項物品　a single product or thing
vocabulary.example_en: More than half of the items in the clothing store were on sale for one day only.

vocabulary.chinese: 達成　to complete a difficult task or reach a goal
vocabulary.example_en: Albert respects those who have accomplished something great in their lives.
Marc felt that leading his basketball team to the city final was his greatest accomplishment of the year.

vocabulary.chinese: [U, C] 疾病　a human, animal, or plant sickness that is usually caused by an infection
vocabulary.example_en: Washing your hands often is a good way to stop the spread of disease.

vocabulary.chinese: 遭感染的　affected by or containing disease-causing bacteria
vocabulary.example_en: Several passengers were found to have the disease after they took the same flight as an infected person.
More than 70 million people have been infected with AIDS in the last 50 years.
The boy developed a serious infection on his leg because he didn’t clean the wound after he fell.

vocabulary.chinese: 找到……（的位置）　to find a certain place, or the position of someone or something
vocabulary.example_en: These days, most taxi drivers use GPS to locate places they don’t know how to drive to.
This hotel is very expensive because it is in a good location right beside a beach.

vocabulary.chinese: [C, U] 獎勵；報酬　something that is given to someone in recognition of a good deed or difficult task he or she has accomplished
vocabulary.example_en: Janice was given a reward of $2,000 for informing the police of the address of the man they were searching for.
The teacher rewarded each of the students with a small prize after they all passed the exam.

vocabulary.chinese: sing. 能力　the power to do a certain thing or a special thing that one can do
vocabulary.example_en: Eagles have the ability to fly without landing for over three hours.

vocabulary.chinese: 預測　to guess that something will happen at a future time
vocabulary.example_en: The weatherperson predicted that it would start raining around 6:00 p.m., and she was correct.

vocabulary.chinese: vi. 設法成功做到；能夠應付　to put great effort into doing something and succeed
vocabulary.example_en: Kyle managed to lose twenty pounds by exercising every day and eating less junk food.

vocabulary.chinese: 明智地使用、管理（金錢、時間等）
vocabulary.example_en: Hailey’s parents told her that she should manage her money more carefully and stop wasting it.
For busy people like the president of a country, good time management is of great importance.

vocabulary.chinese: 創造；產生出　to cause something to come to be or to exist
vocabulary.example_en: The inventor of the telephone, Antonio Meucci, created a new way for humans to communicate.
After the creation of the movie theater’s website, few people called to find out what movies were playing.
Nicole always has interesting new ideas, and she shows that in her creative paintings and drawings.
The students in the class showed great creativity in the wide range of topics they chose for their articles.

vocabulary.chinese: 使接觸；使體驗　to let someone experience something or to introduce something to someone
vocabulary.example_en: By reading these articles, students will be exposed to different opinions about the topic.

vocabulary.chinese: 使暴露於；使遭受（危險或不快）
vocabulary.example_en: It's important to wear sunglasses on sunny days because your eyes could be injured if they are exposed for too long.
Too much exposure to the sun and wind caused the paint on the building to come off.

vocabulary.chinese: 做某事；做某種行為　to carry out an action, task, or duty
vocabulary.example_en: After Mrs. Checknita fell ill, her family hired someone to help her perform daily tasks.
Brad performed a song as a creative way to ask Jennifer to marry him.
The magician will give a performance of his best tricks at the end of the evening show.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 行為　the way a person or animal acts
vocabulary.example_en: Animal behavior can tell us a lot about what a creature is thinking about or wants.
All the guests were impressed by how little Mandy behaved like an adult.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 警示　a warning that there may be some sort of danger or some special problem
vocabulary.example_en: Everybody was asked to leave the mall due to a bomb alert in one of the stores.
The little beeps that your phone makes help you stay alert to new emails, messages, and posts.

vocabulary.chinese: 已完成的；已結束的　finished; entirely done
vocabulary.example_en: The race is not complete until every runner from every team crosses the finish line.
In this English literature course, we will study the complete works of Shakespeare.
Kevin is an excellent student. He always completes school projects long before they are due.

vocabulary.chinese: 指派　to give a special task or role to a certain person
vocabulary.example_en: Craig was assigned the task of giving the new students a tour of the school.
The students are going to Tainan on an assignment for history class.

vocabulary.chinese: 特定的　relating to one certain thing
vocabulary.example_en: There wasn’t any specific reason why Jason didn’t attend the performance with his friends. He just didn’t feel like it.

grammar.explanation: 表「你是否曾⋯⋯？」，用現在完成式的疑問句來詢問對方是否有做某事的經驗。ever一字為加強語氣用，表at any time。

examples.en: Have you ever heard of
examples.ch: 你有聽過地球日嗎？它提醒人們要多做回收、少製造垃圾。

grammar.explanation: 表「這是因為⋯⋯」，說明前面提到之事的原因，亦可寫成That is because S + V。

examples.en: Judy can speak Thai very fluently. This is because she has lived in Thailand for ten years.
examples.ch: Judy能說流利的泰語。這是因為她已在泰國住了十年。

grammar.explanation: 為五種感官的「⋯⋯覺」：sense of smell/taste/touch/hearing/sight（嗅覺／味覺／觸覺／聽覺／視覺）。另外，sense of N也可表示「⋯⋯感」，表了解或判斷某事的能力，或對某事的感覺，如：sense of humor/justice/ direction/responsibility（幽默／正義／方向／責任感）。

examples.en: How can you sleep in such a stinky room? Are you sure there is no problem with your sense of smell ?
examples.ch: 你怎麼能睡在這麼臭的房間裡？你確定你的嗅覺沒有問題？

examples.en: It’s impossible for Milton to become a tour guide since he has no sense of direction at all.
examples.ch: Milton不可能成為導遊，因為他完全沒有方向感。

grammar.explanation: 在此為副詞，可用來修飾比較級。除此之外，a lot/much/ even/still/yet/a little等字詞亦可修飾比較級。

examples.en: The school trip seemed far more interesting than the students had expected.
examples.ch: 這次校外教學似乎比學生們預期的要有趣得多。

grammar.explanation: 表示「因為……原因」。

examples.en: Roxanne decided to skip the party for personal reasons and spend a quiet evening at home.
examples.ch: Roxanne因個人原因決定不參加聚會，而在家度過一個安靜的夜晚。

grammar.explanation: 被藏起來的）為過去分詞當形容詞用，修飾drugs。分詞可以當形容詞用：--過去分詞（p.p.）表示「被動」或「已完成的」。現在分詞（V-ing）則表示「主動的」或「進行中的」。

examples.en: Jill’s foot was cut by a broken vase that had fallen off the shelf.
examples.ch: Jill的腳被從櫃子上掉下來的破碎花瓶劃傷了。

examples.en: Because of the coming/approaching typhoon, people in dangerous areas were told to leave their homes as quickly as possible.
examples.ch: 由於即將來臨的颱風，危險地區的人們被告知盡快離開家園。

grammar.explanation: 加複數可數名詞，表「一些」，義近some，為肯定語氣。a little與a few同義，但後加不可數名詞。若是few或little則表示「幾乎沒有⋯⋯」，為否定的語氣。

examples.en: Taylor has a few friends who like to play badminton, so sometimes they play together after school.
examples.ch: Taylor有一些喜歡打羽球的朋友，所以他們有時候會在放學後一起打球。

examples.en: Since there are few mistakes in the article, there is no need to rewrite it.
examples.ch: 因為這篇文章幾乎沒有錯誤，沒有必要重寫。

grammar.explanation: 為從屬連接詞，表「一旦」。

examples.en: Once you have told the manager your decision, you can’t change your mind.
examples.ch: 一旦你把你的決定告訴經理，就不能再改變心意了。

grammar.explanation: 為從屬連接詞，引導從屬子句their trainer... a reward，表示「某動作持續直到某時或某事發生（為止）」。until亦可當介系詞，後面加上時間點。

examples.en: Alex studied diligently until midnight to prepare for the exam scheduled for the next morning.
examples.ch: Alex認真讀書直到半夜，為第二天早上的考試做準備。

grammar.explanation: 此為授予動詞，表「遞給；拿給某人某物」可用hand + sb. + sth. 或hand + sth. + to + sb.。

examples.en: The soup is too plain. Could you please hand me the black pepper?
examples.ch: 這湯味道太淡了。可以請你把黑胡椒拿給我嗎？

grammar.explanation: 為「（用手或鏟子等工具）將……挖出」之義。三態為dig-dug-dug。

examples.en: With a flashlight in hand, Amy tried to dig out her old photos from the clutter in the dark basement.
examples.ch: Amy拿著手電筒，試著從黑暗地下室的雜物堆裡翻出她的舊照。

grammar.explanation: 為從屬連接詞，表「因為；由於」。=because

examples.en: Jasmine decided to break up with her boyfriend since he seemed to care more about his cell phone than about her.
examples.ch: Jasmine決定跟她的男朋友分手，因為他似乎關心他的手機比關心她還多。

grammar.explanation: 表「邀請某人做某事」。

examples.en: George will invite his neighbors to the barbecue in his backyard this weekend.
examples.ch: 這週末George將邀請他的鄰居來他家後院參加燒烤派對。

grammar.explanation: 為介系詞，表「有著⋯⋯」。此句也可寫成...trees which/that have brown root rot disease。

examples.en: Mark adopted a puppy with black spots and a kitten with blue eyes from an animal shelter.
examples.ch: Mark從動物收容所領養了一隻有著黑色圓點的小狗和一隻有著藍眼睛的小貓。

grammar.explanation: 表「被用來做某事」，也可寫成be used to + VR。

examples.en: This essential oil can be used for relaxing your stiff muscles.
examples.ch: 這精油可以用來放鬆你僵硬的肌肉。

grammar.explanation: 為一複合形容詞，原句應為Maria Tello, who is 58 years old, ...「數字 + 名詞（+ 形容詞）」組合而成的複合形容詞，除了用連字號（hyphen）連起來外，原來的複數名詞因成為形容詞的一部分，須改為單數。

examples.en: My parents planned a relaxing three-day trip to the mountains to escape the city’s hustle and bustle.
examples.ch: 我父母計劃了一趟為期三天的輕鬆山間之旅，以逃離城市的喧囂。

grammar.explanation: 表「藉由做⋯⋯（達到目的或得到結果）」。此處可寫成With its powerful nose, ...。

examples.en: By using her magic power, the witch turned the prince into a toad within a second.
examples.ch: 巫婆藉由她的魔力，在一秒之內把王子變成了癩蛤蟆。

grammar.formula: It turned out that S + V

examples.en: We all thought there would be a pop quiz in biology class today. However,
examples.ch: 我們都以為今天生物課會有隨堂小考。但結果老師請了病假。

grammar.explanation: 表「以這樣的方式」，in在此可省略。

examples.en: How could you talk to your mom in this way ? It’s very rude.
examples.ch: 你怎麼能用這種方式跟你媽媽說話？這樣非常沒有禮貌。

grammar.explanation: 表「某一種」之義，kind亦可用type、sort代替。

examples.en: Aboriginal people use a certain kind of herbal plant to season this dish.
examples.ch: 原住民使用某一種香草植物來調味這道料理。

5.title: Lesson 5The Life of a Plastic1 Bag

vocabulary.chinese: 塑膠（製）的　made of a type of material that can be shaped into countless forms
vocabulary.example_en: You should never put hot drinking water into plastic bottles or cups.
In the old days, many toys were made of wood or metal, but now most of them are made of plastic.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 便利　the condition of being useful, easy, or suitable for people
vocabulary.example_en: Many shops post their business hours on their Facebook pages for the convenience of the public.

vocabulary.chinese: 渴望地　in a way that shows excitement about something that will soon happen
vocabulary.example_en: Rick is eagerly waiting for his boss to post the summer work schedule because he wants to plan his vacation.
After John sent the romantic email to his girlfriend, he was eager for her reply.

vocabulary.chinese: 圍繞　to be or go totally around something or someone
vocabulary.example_en: The house is surrounded by tall trees, so nobody can see it from the road.
The hotel is in beautiful natural surroundings far away from the city.

vocabulary.chinese: 漂流　to move in a certain direction smoothly and slowly, usually on water or in the air
vocabulary.example_en: After the captain fell asleep, the ship passed the harbor and then drifted toward the middle of the sea.

vocabulary.chinese: 羨慕　to desire the same qualities, possessions, opportunities, etc. that someone else has
vocabulary.example_en: Christopher really envies his best friend, Patty, because she has been to so many countries.
Kyle looked with envy at his friend’s smartphone since it was much newer and better than his.

vocabulary.chinese: 擠滿的；擁擠的　filled with things or people
vocabulary.example_en: The Smith family is moving into a new house tomorrow, so their living room is crowded with boxes.
The singer performed her three newest songs in front of a crowd of excited fans.

vocabulary.chinese: 顯然　used to indicate that something is very easy to see or understand
vocabulary.example_en: The little girl is wearing yellow shoes, a yellow dress, and has painted her nails yellow. Obviously, she likes this color.
It was quite obvious to Carrie’s friends that she was in love with Mike because she couldn’t stop talking about him.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 產品　something that is grown or produced, and usually sold
vocabulary.example_en: This shop sells a variety of health and food products for babies.
This company produces more computers than any other company in the world.
Taiwan is known for the production of high-quality bicycles like those that are made by Giant.

vocabulary.chinese: 吃喝　to eat or drink something
vocabulary.example_en: Driving after you consume too much beer or wine can lead to a car crash.
Finishing his assignments consumes most of Riley’s free time in the evening and on the weekend.
The study found that our city is the largest consumer of water in the country.

vocabulary.chinese: 有毒的　able to make a person or animal die or become ill if consumed or entering the body
vocabulary.example_en: Chocolate is delicious for most people, but did you know that it can be poisonous to dogs?
Andy tried to poison the cockroaches in his apartment with a chemical he bought at the store, but it didn’t work.
Nobody except for the scientists is allowed to enter the testing room because there are several deadly poisons in there.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 物質　a kind of solid, liquid, or gas that has certain qualities
vocabulary.example_en: The police found a chemical substance at the murder scene and are doing tests on it right now.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 回收　a process of treating used items such as plastic, metal, glass, etc. so that they can be used again
vocabulary.example_en: After the party, Nick took all the empty bottles out for recycling.
Many people don’t realize that about 75% of the waste they produce can be recycled.
The cleaners were careful not to do any harm to the objects in the museum as they cleaned around them.
Rebecca put the puppy down carefully in order not to harm it.
Looking at the sun for too long is harmful to your eyes.

vocabulary.chinese: sing. 自然環境　the natural world in which living things exist
vocabulary.example_en: It is every person’s responsibility to save the earth’s natural environment for our sons and daughters.
All company owners should create a safe working environment for their employees.
The president’s speech to the students will focus on environmental problems such as shrinking forests and climate change.

vocabulary.chinese: 適當地　in the correct or most suitable manner
vocabulary.example_en: Marc didn’t complete the assignment properly, so the teacher asked him to do it again.
Silly jokes should only be told at the proper place and time.

grammar.explanation: 在此為使役動詞，句構為let + O + VR，解釋為「讓……」。

examples.en: The coach let the boy swim in the shallow end of the pool to build his confidence in the water.
examples.ch: 教練讓男孩在泳池的淺水區游泳，以建立他在水裡的信心。

examples.en: It suddenly occurred to me that I had forgotten to print out my report, but it was too late.
examples.ch: 我突然想到我忘記把報告印出來了，但為時已晚。

grammar.explanation: 為形容詞，置於be動詞之後。  同 by oneself, on one’s own

examples.en: After reading Sarah’s letter, I realized that I wasn’t alone .
examples.ch: 讀了Sarah的信之後，我了解到我並不孤單。

grammar.explanation: 當形容詞時也常放在主詞之後，表達only的意思。

examples.en: Money alone cannot make people’s lives colorful and fulfilling.
examples.ch: 單有金錢無法讓人的生命變得精采又令人愉快滿足。

examples.en: Since her husband passed away, Jenny has always had dinner alone .
examples.ch: 自從她先生過世之後，Jenny總是一個人吃晚餐。

examples.en: Since the skies are clear, we could have a picnic in the park tomorrow.
examples.ch: 因為天氣晴朗，我們明天可以在公園裡野餐。

examples.en: More and more students are choosing to take a gap year after graduating from high school. Some do part-time jobs, and others travel abroad.
examples.ch: 愈來愈多的學生在高中畢業之後選擇休一個「空檔年」。有些人去打工，有些人到國外旅行。

grammar.explanation: 在此為連接詞，連接後面的子句；until也可當介系詞，後面接名詞的結構。

examples.en: Tom didn’t show his report card to his mom until she promised not to send him to a military school.
examples.ch: Tom直到媽媽答應不會送他去軍校才把成績單給她看。

examples.en: The poor kid didn’t finish his homework until midnight . (not…until….直到…才…) until/till 直到
examples.ch: 這個可憐的小孩直到深夜才完成作業。

grammar.explanation: 在此當副詞用，意為「那麼」，常用於否定句。

examples.en: Though the area isn’t that dangerous , you’d better bring pepper spray with you.
examples.ch: 雖然這個區域沒有那麼危險，你最好還是隨身攜帶防狼噴霧。

grammar.explanation: 為肯定用法，意思是「一些」，後面加可數複數名詞；若僅有few一字則為否定用法，意思是「很少或幾乎沒有」，後面同樣加可數複數名詞。

examples.en: Only a few seats in the theater remained empty as the show began.
examples.ch: 表演開始的時候，劇場裡只有一些位置還是空著的。

examples.en: Bob is the most selfish person I’ve ever seen. This is why he has few friends .
examples.ch: Bob是我看過最自私的人。這就是為什麼他幾乎沒有朋友。

grammar.explanation: 為「幾天之後」。  同 after a few days

examples.en: Kevin’s puppy went missing last week. Amazingly, it came back on its own
examples.ch: Kevin的小狗上星期走失了。神奇的是，幾天後牠就自己回來了。

examples.en: Mike bought a pair of boots for his hiking trip next week.
examples.ch: Mike為下週的健行買了一雙靴子。

grammar.explanation: 指的是「根據他人的說法」，比較像引述的概念，通常置於句首；若為自己的想法，不可講according to me，而是要用in my opinion。

examples.en: According to this news report, the man who committed the crime has been arrested.
examples.ch: 根據這篇新聞報導，犯下那項罪行的人已經被逮捕了。

grammar.explanation: 可用於以下幾種情況：

examples.en: My sister and her boyfriend are going to watch the latest blockbuster tonight.
examples.ch: 我妹妹和她男友今晚要去看最新的賣座片。

examples.en: The outbreak of COVID-19 has made our daily activities completely different .
examples.ch: 新冠肺炎的爆發已經讓我們的日常活動完全不一樣了。

examples.en: Your encouragement has made me a
examples.ch: 你的鼓勵讓我現今成為一個勇於接受新挑戰的人。

examples.en: I am tired of William’s endless complaints. He seems to be dissatisfied with everything in his life.
examples.ch: 我厭倦了William無止盡的抱怨。他似乎對生活中的一切都感到不滿。

grammar.explanation: 在此當副詞用，表示「到處」；此外，around也可當介系詞用。

examples.en: Children ran around in the park, laughing and playing games in the warm sunshine.
examples.ch: 孩子們在公園裡跑來跑去，在溫暖的陽光下歡笑、玩耍。

6.title: Lesson 6Built for Freedom1: The Statue2 of Liberty*

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 自由　the right to do or say what one wants
vocabulary.example_en: After Simon turned eighteen, his parents gave him the freedom to watch any movies he wanted to.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 雕像　a figure of a person, animal, god, goddess, etc. that is usually made of metal or stone
vocabulary.example_en: The island of Hainan in China has a 108-meter statue of the goddess Kuanyin.

vocabulary.chinese: 移除　to take a thing away from somewhere
vocabulary.example_en: Jasmine chewed gum to remove the smell of garlic from her breath.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 慶祝　the act of doing activities for a special day or event
vocabulary.example_en: Yvonne invited her friends out for dinner in celebration of finishing all of their exams.
Every October 10 our country holds a national celebration to remember an important event in our country’s history.
The whole country celebrated when their team won the FIFA World Cup for the first time.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 獨立　freedom from being controlled by a person, a country, etc.
vocabulary.example_en: India achieved independence from the United Kingdom on August 15, 1947.
The Philippines has been independent from Spain for over 100 years, but about 3 million people there still speak Spanish.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 紀念碑　a building, statue, or other large object built to remind us of an important person or event
vocabulary.example_en: The monument was set up to remember Nelson Mandela as a great leader.

vocabulary.chinese: 象徵；代表　to stand for something

vocabulary.example_en: In many cultures, the dove is regarded as a symbol of peace.
The fifty stars on the American flag are symbolic of the country’s fifty states.

vocabulary.chinese: 牢固地；堅定地　in a strong way that will prevent something from becoming loose; with a strong will
vocabulary.example_en: Jack firmly closed the lid of the jar so that it wouldn’t open accidentally.
The apples in the basket were all fresh, firm, and perfect for making pie.

vocabulary.chinese: 作為……的象徵；代表　to be a symbol of something
vocabulary.example_en: Sometimes Chinese speakers type “3Q” to represent the words “thank you” because “3Q” sounds like “thank you” in Chinese.
Roberto was chosen to represent his class in the school performance because he is a good actor.
Wendy didn’t like the representation of women in the movie; all of them were shown as being quiet and weak.

vocabulary.chinese: 古代的；古老的　from a period of time in the distant past
vocabulary.example_en: The Great Wall was built thousands of years ago to stop ancient China’s enemies from invading.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 蓄奴制；奴隸身分　the practice of owning and forcing people to work for no pay or the condition of being owned and forced to work for no pay
vocabulary.example_en: This book is about a girl who was sold into slavery and made to work on a farm for years.
When Abraham Lincoln ended slavery in the US, many of his friends still owned slaves.

vocabulary.chinese: 不公正的；不公平的　not equal or fair
vocabulary.example_en: Several players cheated during the game, so everybody agrees that the final score is unfair.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 對待；待遇　the way a person behaves toward or deals with another person or thing
vocabulary.example_en: The prison was closed after many reports revealed the poor treatment of prisoners there.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 遊客　a person who is traveling to or visiting a place for fun or to learn about it
vocabulary.example_en: Mary and John went into the tourist center to ask for information about local hotels.
Tourism in the country grew sharply after it was chosen as this year’s best place to visit by a popular magazine.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 有吸引力的事物或地點　a place that people want to see or an activity that people want to do
vocabulary.example_en: Beaches and water sports are the main attractions in Kenting.
Famous sights such as the Eiffel Tower and Louvre Museum attract over 30 million tourists to Paris every year.
Modern buildings are not attractive to Britney because she thinks they are seldom unique.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 地標　a large natural or man-made thing that makes it easy to know one’s location
vocabulary.example_en: The Tokyo Tower and the Tokyo Skytree are two of Japan’s most famous landmarks.

vocabulary.chinese: sing. 地球；世界　the world (used especially to emphasize its large size and round shape)
vocabulary.example_en: Many of David Bowie’s fans around the globe posted images or words on Facebook and Instagram in memory of him after he died on January 10, 2016.
Many people believe that global warming is causing a higher number of large storms such as typhoons.

grammar.explanation: 在這裡為名詞，用法尚有take/have a look at + N，與動詞look at + N同義。

examples.en: Joyce took a look at the price tag and put the teddy bear back on the shelf.
examples.ch: Joyce看了一下標價，就把那隻泰迪熊放回架上了。

examples.en: The playful puppy is a new addition to the family.
examples.ch: 這隻頑皮小狗狗是這個家庭的新成員。

grammar.formula: which was later named Liberty Island
grammar.explanation: 為形容詞子句，因Bedlore’s Island為專有名詞，具獨一無二之特性，故採非限定用法，which前加逗號。

examples.en: London, which I visited last summer, mesmerized me with its historic charm and modern vibrancy.
examples.ch: 去年夏天我造訪了倫敦，它的歷史魅力和現代活力讓我著迷。

grammar.explanation: 意指「鳴槍」，課文中的鳴槍為慶典中的儀式。

examples.en: As soon as the umpire fired _ a gun , all of the runners started to dash forward.
examples.ch: 裁判一鳴槍，所有的賽跑選手就開始向前奔跑。

grammar.formula: to VR = in order to VR = so as to VR，
grammar.explanation: 表「為了要⋯⋯」，後面所接的原形動詞為想達到的目的或意圖要做的事。
為反身代名詞，意指「本身」，在本句指的是前面的the statue。

examples.en: The computer fixed itself after a quick restart.
examples.ch: 快速重新開機之後，電腦就自己修復了。

grammar.explanation: 高度 + tall/high 表「高度為⋯⋯」。tall表示人或物的高度，high表示物的高度

examples.en: The building stands three hundred meters high/tall , overlooking the bustling cityscape.
examples.ch: 這棟建築物高三百公尺，俯瞰繁華的都市景觀。

grammar.explanation: 意指「隨著時間過去」，如本句所示，此片語常出現在完成式的句子中。

examples.en: Friendships will deepen and become more meaningful over time .
examples.ch: 友誼會隨著時間的推移加深並變得更有意義。

examples.en: Jack has been exposed to art since he was little, and that’s why he is quite familiar with the works of Monet.
examples.ch: Jack從小就接觸美術，這也就是他相當熟悉莫內作品的原因。

grammar.explanation: 表「如此⋯⋯以致於⋯⋯」，前後語意為因果關係，用於凸顯事情到某種程度後所產生的結果。

examples.en: Sam runs so fast/quickly that no one can catch up with him.
examples.ch: Sam跑得如此快以致於沒有人可以追得上他。

examples.en: “Why does the sky turn red and purple during sunrise and sunset?” asked a five-year-old boy.
examples.ch: 一個五歲的小男孩問：「為什麼日出和日落的時候，天空會變成紅色和紫

grammar.explanation: 表「⋯⋯的每一個⋯⋯」，接單數動詞。

examples.en: Each page of the book has big pictures of different animals so that readers can take a closer look.
examples.ch: 這本書的每一頁都有不同動物的大張圖片，以便讀者就能更仔細地看。

examples.en: Joan handed me a note with a single word —“lucky”—written on it in elegant calligraphy.
examples.ch: Joan遞給我一張紙條，上面用優雅的書法字寫著單一個字「吉」。

examples.en: It was in this room that I found my stolen diamond ring.
examples.ch: 我就是在這間房間裡找到我被偷走的鑽石戒指。

examples.en: It was last Friday that my baby girl was born.
examples.ch: 我的寶貝女兒就是在上週五出生的。

examples.en: There is still a long way to success . Only those who persist can reach their destination.
examples.ch: 離成功還有很長一段路要走。只有堅持的人才能抵達終點。

grammar.explanation: 斷掉的；破掉的。此字為break的過去分詞，作為形容詞用。

examples.en: Greg asked his elder brother to fix his robot’s broken arm
examples.ch: Greg要求他的哥哥幫他修機器人斷掉的手臂。

examples.en: Every time my sister sits on the sofa, the cat lies at
examples.ch: 每次我姐姐坐在沙發上的時候，那隻貓就會躺在她的腳邊陪伴她。

grammar.explanation: 因此  同 thereforethus為副詞而非連接詞，故不能連接兩個字詞、片語或子句，通常其前須加and。若要連接兩個子句，也可在thus前加分號

examples.en: I was wasting too much time going through my friends’ updates on Instagram;
examples.ch: 我浪費太多時間看朋友們在IG的動態更新；因此，我決定把這個應用程式從我的手機移除。

grammar.explanation: 表「免於⋯⋯」、「不受⋯⋯影響」，後面通常接造成限制或痛苦的事物。

examples.en: Yoga and meditation brought Mandy freedom from stress and anxiety.
examples.ch: 瑜珈和冥想讓Mandy擺脫了壓力和焦慮。

grammar.formula: It is such a popular landmark that every visitor to New York City...
grammar.explanation: 為such...that...句型，意指「如此⋯⋯以致於⋯⋯」。such為形容詞，後接名詞或名詞片語，接單數可數名詞時，需加不定冠詞a(n)，再接形容詞

examples.en: The 1000-piece puzzle was such a ___ ___challenge that it took me days to solve.
examples.ch: 這個1,000片的拼圖非常具有挑戰性，以致於我花了幾天的時間才解決。

examples.en: During our vacation to Paris, we plan to pay a visit to the Louvre.
examples.ch: 在巴黎度假期間，我們打算去參觀羅浮宮。

vocabulary.chinese: [U, C] 勝利；成就　a great success or achievement
vocabulary.example_en: The soccer team’s moment of triumph came when the final goal was scored.

vocabulary.word: The success of the pop star’s new album is a triumph over critics who said she would never make great music agai
vocabulary.example_en: When the actor was presented with the award, he smiled in triumph and waved to the audience.

vocabulary.chinese: 申請；報名　(applied—applied—applying) to officially request something, such as a job or a place at a university
vocabulary.example_en: To apply for the job, Mary had to fill out several forms and send them to the company.
In Joy’s letter of application, she explained why she wanted to study at the university.
suitable applicant for the position of manager will be someone with strong leadership and communication skills.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 樂器　an item whose main use is to make music, such as a guitar or violin
vocabulary.example_en: Harry can play several instruments, such as the piano and the trumpet.
In our testing center, we use many different scientific instruments to do our experiments.

vocabulary.chinese: 以某種方式；不知怎麼地　in a manner that is not clear or cannot be easily understood
vocabulary.example_en: When Gina lost her keys, her dog somehow knew exactly where to find them.
Justin and Kate could barely believe their ears when the doctor told them they were going to have twins.
It is traditional in Thailand to enter temples in bare feet or socks.

vocabulary.chinese: 非常寶貴的　very important or useful
vocabulary.example_en: Eric’s grandma has a lot of life experience, so her advice is invaluable to him.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 鼓勵；鼓舞　words or actions that make a person feel more confident about doing something
vocabulary.example_en: My mom’s encouragement helped me feel less nervous before the school play.
Sarah’s friends encouraged her to improve her health by jogging every morning and eating more fruit and vegetables.
Because Lisa’s parents worried about her safety, they discouraged her from staying out late at night.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 音樂會　a musical performance or show given in a public place
vocabulary.example_en: Tom and Karen went to a classical concert to celebrate one year of marriage.

vocabulary.chinese: 收到；獲得　to be sent or given something
vocabulary.example_en: Andy received a pair of gloves from his girlfriend as a Christmas gift.

vocabulary.chinese: 使讚嘆；使傾倒　to amaze someone with a particularly impressive quality
vocabulary.example_en: During his performance, the guitar player dazzled the audience with his unbelievable skills.
While Jerry was driving at night, the lights from an oncoming car dazzled him, and he almost had an accident.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 潛力　qualities that could be developed and bring a person success
vocabulary.example_en: With the help of their new coach, the baseball players are able to reach their full potential in games.
This report shows that the potential for traffic accidents increases on rainy days.
Self-driving cars pose potential dangers, including technical problems and difficulties in understanding road situations.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 尖叫　a loud high cry one makes when he or she is hurt, frightened, excited, etc.
vocabulary.example_en: The children let out screams of delight during the roller-coaster ride.
Nancy screamed in terror when she was shaken awake by a powerful earthquake in the middle of the night.

vocabulary.chinese: 頭暈目眩的　feeling that you are losing your balance and that everything is spinning around
vocabulary.example_en: When my favorite band announced that they were coming to perform in my hometown, I felt dizzy with excitement.

vocabulary.chinese: 在海外；到海外　in or to a foreign country, usually one across an ocean
vocabulary.example_en: After Tina quit her job, she lived overseas in South America for a year to experience life in a different culture.
Many overseas students come to this university to study and make friends from all over the world.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 決心　the quality that makes someone keep trying to achieve something difficult
vocabulary.example_en: Kevin showed strong determination to improve his swimming skills by taking lessons every week and practicing hard in his free time.
The meeting will determine when we can start hiring new workers.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 信心；信任　trust in something or confidence that someone will do something specific
vocabulary.example_en: Although we were losing at halftime, our teacher had faith in our ability to turn the game around and win.
At their wedding, Nick and Fiona promised to remain faithful to each other forever.

vocabulary.chinese: 追求；從事　to do something or seek to achieve something, often over a long period
vocabulary.example_en: Harriet practices very hard to pursue her goal of becoming a successful dancer.
The police officers pursued the thief through the streets of the city until they caught him.
Many young people are leaving this poor village in pursuit of a better job.

grammar.explanation: 謀生後可加V-ing來進一步說明謀生的方式，如make a living delivering packages「送包裹維生」

examples.en: That middle-aged man works extremely hard every single day at his job to make a living for himself.
examples.ch: 那位中年男子每天都非常努力地工作為自己謀生。

examples.en: I’m really looking forward to the carnival which is scheduled to take place in Venice next month. Ex:I’m looking forward to seeing you soon.
examples.ch: 我真的很期待預計下個月在威尼斯舉辦的嘉年華。

examples.en: Let me show you step-by-step exactly how to properly change a flat tire.
examples.ch: 讓我一步步向您展示如何正確更換漏氣的輪胎。

examples.en: The crowd was carried away by the speaker’s passionate speech.
examples.ch: 那位講者充滿激情的演講讓群眾興奮不已。

grammar.explanation: 表「因為」

examples.en: Since the power was out and there was no way to keep the house lit or heated, we went straight to bed early last night.
examples.ch: 因為停電了，房子也無法照明或供暖，我們昨天早早就上床睡覺了。

examples.en: Jim is really good at solving the most complex mathematical a. 數學的/mathematics n. 數學problems. He is the most intelligent person that I’ve ever met.
examples.ch: Jim非常擅長解決最複雜的數學問題。他是我所見過最聰明的人。

grammar.explanation: 在此為複合關係代名詞，等於the thing(s) that或the thing(s) which。

examples.en: The best tip to being happy that I’ve ever heard is to do what makes you truly fulfilled in every aspect of your life.
examples.ch: 我所聽過保持快樂的最佳訣竅就是在生活各方面做會讓你真正滿足的事情。

grammar.explanation: 表示「藉由……」。

examples.en: By doing regular exercise and getting enough sleep, my mother stays perfectly fit and healthy.
examples.ch: 藉由定期運動和睡眠充足，我母親保持完美的身材和健康。

grammar.explanation: 表示「設法做到……」。

examples.en: With perseverance and hard work, Star finally managed to showcase her artwork at the 13th Grammy Awards.
examples.ch: 憑藉著堅持和努力，Star終於設法在第13屆葛萊美獎上展現她的藝術作品。

grammar.explanation: 表「起初；一開始」。

examples.en: At first , the new video game seemed too complicated, but after I had played it for a while, it became easier to understand.
examples.ch: 起初，這個新遊戲看起來太複雜了，但我玩了一段時間後，它就變得容易理解了。

grammar.explanation: 複數可數名詞，表示「一些；幾個」；若是few則表「幾乎沒有⋯⋯」，為否定語氣。

examples.en: After the board members worked through a few complicated problems, they finally reached a consensus and carried out the project with confidence.
examples.ch: 董事會成員在解決了幾個複雜的問題後，終於達成共識，滿懷信心地進行此專案。

grammar.explanation: 表示「持續做……」。

examples.en: Vicky kept asking____ questions in class until she fully understood everything.
examples.ch: Vicky在課堂中不斷地提出問題，直到她完全理解所有內容。

examples.en: High school students shine with potential, just as they brighten the world with their enthusiasm and creativity.
examples.ch: 高中生閃耀著潛力，就像他們用熱情和創造力照亮了世界。

grammar.explanation: 表「最後成為；最後處於」。

examples.en: After taking many wrong turns and detours, we ended up lost in the town.
examples.ch: 在走錯很多路並兜圈子之後，我們最終在城裡迷路了。

examples.en: During a trip, no matter how hard we try to avoid them, sometimes we will still end up in unexpected situations. Isn’t this why traveling is so fascinating?
examples.ch: 在旅行中，無論我們如何努力避免，有時還是會遇到意想不到的情況。這就是為什麼旅行如此令人著迷，不是嗎？

examples.en: Through their shared love for music, the band members discovered a language that transcended barriers and united hearts from different corners of the world.
examples.ch: 透過他們對音樂的共同熱愛，樂團成員發現了一種超越障礙並團結來自世界不同角落的心靈的語言。

examples.en: With his exceptional problem-solving skills, Tommy is able to tackle complex challenges precisely and with ease.
examples.ch: 憑藉出色的解決問題能力，Tommy能夠輕鬆地精確應對複雜的挑戰。

vocabulary.chinese: 熟悉的　well-known to someone, usually because that thing or person has often been seen or heard
vocabulary.example_en: Japanese cartoon characters such as Doraemon, Pikachu, and Totoro are familiar to kids all over the world.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 衝動　a strong desire to do something
vocabulary.example_en: Jason had an urge to change his daily routine, so he went swimming after work instead of going straight home.
Many people were urging the country’s leaders to do a better job of helping the homeless.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 策略　a plan or method used to achieve a certain aim
vocabulary.example_en: The salesman’s strategy for closing a deal is to describe the features that make his products better than other companies’.

vocabulary.chinese: 看得見的　able to be seen
vocabulary.example_en: In the sky there are 9,096 stars that are visible to the naked eye.
Many people like to purchase goods inside international airports because they don’t need to pay taxes.
It is the café’s policy that each customer must make a purchase of at least one food or drink item.

vocabulary.chinese: 有利潤的　(used for a product or business activity) able to make money
vocabulary.example_en: The locals that live near this harbor have opened several highly profitable seafood restaurants.
The students really profited from the teacher’s story because they had a good laugh and learned something new.
The couple hope to make a profit by selling their house for much more than they paid for it last year.

vocabulary.chinese: 同樣地；相似地　used to indicate that two or more facts, actions, statements, etc. are almost the same
vocabulary.example_en: Many Arctic birds fly south when winter approaches. Similarly, monarch butterflies fly to warmer places during the cold season.
These two kinds of fish are similar in appearance but taste quite different.
When Blake traveled to foreign countries, he noticed the similarity of all people rather than what made them different.

vocabulary.chinese: 針對；對象是　to direct something, such as information, a product, or some action, at a certain person or group of people
vocabulary.example_en: This picture book is aimed primarily at children under the age of five.
The actress likes to watch videos of her performances with the aim of improving her acting skills.

vocabulary.chinese: [U, C] 衝動　a sudden urge to do something, usually without thinking about the possible results
vocabulary.example_en: Kate bought an expensive pair of shoes on impulse but later realized she didn’t need them.

vocabulary.chinese: 展示；陳列　to put something somewhere so people can easily see it
vocabulary.example_en: The scooter shop displays some of its most popular models on the front sidewalk to attract potential buyers.
Some of Picasso’s less famous works will be on display at the Museum of Modern Art this spring.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 特價　a discounted price for something, usually for a short period of time
vocabulary.example_en: The buy-two-get-one-free offer on all baked items at this bakery will only last until October 31.
The coach offered the best words of encouragement he could think of to each member of the team.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 顧客；客戶　a person or company that buys goods or services from a shop or business
vocabulary.example_en: Mrs. Stewart is used to dealing with angry people because she handles all the customer complaints for a large store.

vocabulary.chinese: [U, C] 優勢；有利條件　something that helps one be better or more successful than others
vocabulary.example_en: It would be to the company’s advantage to promote their new product because the public is not familiar with it.

vocabulary.chinese: 最後　in the end, usually after a long period of time or many difficulties
vocabulary.example_en: Raymond failed the GEPT the first time, but he tried again and eventually got an excellent score.
Good training and a positive attitude led to Nicole’s eventual success as a designer.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 圈套　a plan to trick people, either by catching them or by making them do something they don’t want to
vocabulary.example_en: When the boy acted like he was crying, the mother fell into his trap and bought the cookies for him.
tree that fell in front of the main door trapped the people inside the burning building until help arrived.

vocabulary.chinese: 知道；意識到　knowing or realizing something
vocabulary.example_en: None of the students was aware that their teacher was actually a famous author.
The main aim of World AIDS Day is to raise our awareness of AIDS.

vocabulary.chinese: 聰明的　showing or done in a way that shows intelligent thinking
vocabulary.example_en: The teacher was really impressed with the students’ clever ideas in their reports.

grammar.explanation: 表「某人發生某事」。表「發生」的用法還有：occur、take place，這些都只能用主動，沒有被動用法。

examples.en: Some basketball fans around the world still can’t believe that the tragedy that
examples.ch: 世界各地的一些籃球迷至今仍無法相信發生在Kobe Bryant身上的悲劇是真的。

examples.en: Allie picked up lots of groceries that were not on her shopping list.
examples.ch: Allie買了許多不在購物清單上的雜貨。

examples.en: Bonnie picked up a box from the ground to see what was hidden inside.
examples.ch: Bonnie從地上撿起一個盒子來看看裡面藏了什麼。

examples.en: If my father has time to pick me up from the cram school, I won’t need to take a bus.
examples.ch: 如果我爸有時間去補習班接我，我就不用搭公車。

examples.en: Three days later , the package finally arrived, much to our relief.
examples.ch: 三天之後，包裹終於到達了，讓我們鬆了一口氣。

examples.en: Whenever I think of hiking , I imagine myself surrounded by nature’s beauty and tranquility.
examples.ch: 每當我想到健行時，我會想像自己被大自然的美麗和寧靜所包圍。

grammar.explanation: 使⋯⋯make為使役動詞，後面的受詞補語若與受詞為主動的關係，則用原形動詞

examples.en: Barbie’s mom made her clean her room before her friends came over for a sleepover.
examples.ch: Barbie的媽媽要她在朋友來過夜之前打掃房間。

grammar.explanation: 所有格 + 複數名詞表「⋯⋯之一」，用來指許多人、事、物當中的一個；當主詞時，其主詞是one，所以動詞為單數。

examples.en: One of the girls who is gossiping in the classroom is Sunny.
examples.ch: 其中一個在教室裡聊八卦的女生就是Sunny.

grammar.explanation: 比較級 + S + V, the + 比較級 + S + V，表「愈⋯⋯愈⋯⋯」。

examples.en: The longer I stay in Taitung, the more I enjoy the life here.
examples.ch: 我在臺東待得愈久，我就愈喜歡這裡的生活。

examples.en: It’s difficult for the child to solve such complex problems without proper guidance.
examples.ch: 如果沒有適當的指導，那個孩子很難解出這麼複雜的數學題。

examples.en: Make sure (that) you lock the front door before leaving the house.
examples.ch: 離開房子前，確保你有鎖上前門。

grammar.explanation: 表示正在討論的人、事、物中沒有特別指定的「另一個」。當形容詞時，其後必須接單數名詞及單數動詞；當代名詞時，則直接加單數動詞。

examples.en: The boots I like are sold out, so I have to choose ____another____ pair.
examples.ch: 我喜歡的靴子賣完了，所以我必須選擇另一雙。

grammar.explanation: 「引人注目的」為「名詞 + 分詞」的複合形容詞。對修飾的對象而言，複合形容詞中的動詞若為主動，會使用現在分詞；若為被動，則使用過去分詞。
表「使⋯⋯的」。make是不完全及物動詞，在受詞後加形容詞作為受詞補語。

examples.en: To ___make___ his speech ___more___ ___interesting___, the speaker quoted a famous comedian.
examples.ch: 為了要讓他的演講更有趣，那位講者引用了一位知名喜劇演員說過的話。

grammar.explanation: 為add A to B「把A加到B」的被動用法。

examples.en: If you insist on adding these sentences to the article, remember to check whether the article as a whole is still coherent.
examples.ch: 如果你堅持要把這些句子加到文章裡，記得要檢查整篇文章是否仍連貫。

grammar.explanation: 引導名詞子句作為show的受詞，that可以被省略。

examples.en: I believe (that) creativity is the key to surviving in the era of artificial intelligence.
examples.ch: 我相信創意是在人工智慧時代生存的關鍵。

examples.en: Ramon reminded his son to take his medicine after meals by
examples.ch: Ramon藉由傳送語音訊息提醒他的兒子飯後要記得吃藥。

examples.en: Let’s hang out at the park this weekend and enjoy the sunny weather together.
examples.ch: 讓我們這個週末一起去公園打發時間，享受陽光明媚的天氣。

examples.en: The meal that I recommend ___starts___ ____off____ ____with____ a Caesar salad, which is then followed by onion soup.
examples.ch: 我推薦的菜首先是凱薩沙拉，接著是洋蔥湯。

grammar.explanation: 表「變得⋯⋯」。grow為連綴動詞，後接形容詞作主詞補語。連綴動詞表示「變得」，尚有become、turn、get、come、go等。

examples.en: I haven’t seen Brian for years; he has grown taller and stronger over time.
examples.ch: 我好幾年沒見到Brian了。隨著時間過去，他變得更高、更壯了。

examples.en: If you keep being so self-centered, you will end up
examples.ch: 如果你繼續這樣以自我為中心，最後會落得一個朋友都沒有。

vocabulary.chinese: 詳細的；細緻的　having features or a lot of information; paying a lot of attention to features or information
vocabulary.example_en: The art college students were asked to write a detailed description of their favorite work of art.
Zushi Temple in Sanxia, New Taipei City is famous for the details on its walls and roofs.

vocabulary.chinese: sing. 凌亂；髒亂　a dirty or disorganized condition in a certain place
vocabulary.example_en: week after the typhoon left the country, many areas were in a mess.
The teacher refused to accept Brian’s report because his handwriting was too messy.

vocabulary.chinese: 表達　to share a feeling or idea using words, actions, artworks, etc.
vocabulary.example_en: Lady Gaga uses music to express her view that people are different but equal.
Freedom of expression means that we can say anything, but this should not include words that harm others.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 想法；意見　one’s ideas or views about someone or something, which may or may not be true
vocabulary.example_en: In Cassandra’s opinion, borrowing books from the library is smarter than buying them because we usually only read them once.

vocabulary.chinese: 對比；對照　to compare two things by showing their differences
vocabulary.example_en: The video contrasted a picture of a heavy smoker’s lungs with that of a non-smoker’s to discourage people from smoking.
There are many contrasts between my two favorite movies; one is a light musical and the other is a serious drama.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 可怕；恐怖　something that causes fear, shock, or disgust
vocabulary.example_en: It’s difficult for most of us to imagine the horror of war because we haven’t experienced it ourselves.
The kids watched in horror as the lions tore the deer to pieces on a wildlife TV show.
The whole world was shocked by the horrible news that the World Trade Center in New York was under attack.
It horrified Orlando to find out how much garbage is floating around in the ocean.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 無辜；純真　the state of not having much knowledge or experience of the world, especially of adult matters or bad things
vocabulary.example_en: The salesman was good at getting customers to buy his products because he had a look of innocence.
The young travelers were so innocent that they didn’t realize they needed to lock their hotel room.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 暴力　behavior or actions that are meant to hurt or kill a person or other living things
vocabulary.example_en: Most people would agree that the use of violence against children is not right in any situation.
The football player was removed from the game due to his repeated violent behavior on the field.

vocabulary.chinese: 視覺的　related to seeing or sight
vocabulary.example_en: Debra loves all kinds of visual arts, including pottery, paintings, drawings, and films.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 汙染　the process or state of air, water, soil, etc. becoming or being dirty
vocabulary.example_en: This machine can check the level of air pollution in your apartment and help reduce it.
The seaside factory was polluting the ocean with poisonous chemicals.

vocabulary.chinese: [U] 許可；准許　the act of letting someone do something, usually in a formal or official way
vocabulary.example_en: Excuse me, sir. You need to get special permission for the video camera in the museum.
When the seat belt signs are on, passengers on the airplane are not permitted to stand up.

vocabulary.chinese: [U, C] 努力　the physical or mental energy required to do something
vocabulary.example_en: Cassandra put a lot of effort into her science project and her teacher praised it in the end.

vocabulary.chinese: [C] 社區　a place and all the people who live there
vocabulary.example_en: Leaders of various local communities are meeting today to express their ideas about how to clean up their streets.

vocabulary.chinese: 聯想　to make a connection between two things or people in your mind
vocabulary.example_en: Most people in Taiwan associate Beethoven’s “For Elise” with the coming of the garbage truck.
In order to read, a person must be able to make an association between letters and the sounds they represent.

vocabulary.chinese: 犯罪的　connected with or involving things that are against the law
vocabulary.example_en: Studies show that better education can lead to a drop in criminal behavior.
The police are searching the community for a criminal who just beat up two people for no reason.
Taiwan has one of the lowest rates of violent crime in the world.

vocabulary.chinese: 破壞性的；毀滅性的　causing a lot of damage
vocabulary.example_en: It is hard for most people to imagine the destructive power of modern bombs.
The strong winds completely destroyed the wooden fence around the farmer’s land.
According to this report, air pollution could result in the destruction of many ancient pieces of art someday.

grammar.explanation: 從⋯⋯到⋯⋯（表範圍或幅度）

examples.en: You can find a wide variety of accessories in this store, from necklaces and earrings to hair pins and sunglasses.
examples.ch: 你在這間店裡可以找到各式各樣的飾品，從項鍊和耳環到髮夾和太陽眼鏡

grammar.explanation: 表「有些⋯⋯有些⋯⋯」。用於一群人或物，有三種以上情況，但只列舉兩種。= some….some…..

examples.en: When Selina walked into the library, some people were preparing for exams, and others were watching movies on their laptops.
examples.ch: 當Selina走進圖書館時，有些人在準備考試，有些人在用筆電看電影。

grammar.explanation: 為連接詞，用來連接兩個語意對比的子句。  同 whereas

examples.en: My parents went to a fancy restaurant to celebrate Valentine’s Day last night, while/whereas I had instant noodles at home by myself. It’s so unfair!
examples.ch: 我爸媽昨晚去高檔餐廳慶祝西洋情人節，而我卻一個人在家裡吃泡麵。真是不公平！

grammar.explanation: 為副詞，為「顯然地」。  同 clearly, apparently

examples.en: Obviously , Erica is out on a date with someone; I sent her a message hours ago, but she hasn’t read it.
examples.ch: Erica顯然正在外跟某個人約會；我幾個小時前就傳訊息給她了，但她都還沒讀。

grammar.explanation: 為名詞，為「勤奮；努力；用功」。
為動詞片語，為「努力地工作／讀書」。
為形容詞，為「辛勤的；努力的；用功的」。

examples.en: Tony is such a poor young man; his hard work didn’t lead to success in the end.
examples.ch: Tony真是可憐的年輕人；他的勤奮最後並未導致成功。

examples.en: Nancy has worked hard to provide for her family since she graduated from college.
examples.ch: Nancy自從大學畢業之後，就一直努力地工作養家。

grammar.explanation: 像…… like為介系詞，為「像」，故後接名詞。

examples.en: The girl’s smile is like a ray of sunshine that raises everyone’s spirits.
examples.ch: 這女孩的笑容像一縷振奮大家精神的陽光。

examples.en: Mary apologized for the mistake, and she said that it was unintentional.
examples.ch: Mary為這個錯誤道歉，說這是無意的。

grammar.explanation: 可作對等連接詞，連接與其前方同一詞性的字詞。也可作介系詞片語。  同 instead of

examples.en: Yesterday Lucy bought some potted herbs from a flower market rather
examples.ch: Lucy昨天在花市買了一些香草盆栽，沒有去健身房。

grammar.explanation: 為表主格的關係代名詞，用以修飾先行詞artist，也可以用that取代。而此處形容詞子句可改為分詞片語，即省略who/that，將其後的動詞paint改為表主動的現在分詞painting。

examples.en: The journalist who/that is proposing the question seems to work for the mainstream media.
examples.ch: 正在提出問題的那位記者似乎在主流媒體工作。

grammar.explanation: 此處表示「作品；著作」為可數名詞。表「工作」時，為不可數名詞。

examples.en: The museum displayed a collection of works by renowned artists.
examples.ch: 那間博物館展出了知名藝術家一系列的藝術作品。

examples.en: The art teacher asked his students to contrast works from the Renaissance period with those from the Baroque period.
examples.ch: 美術老師要學生將文藝復興時期的作品和巴洛克時期的作品做對比。

grammar.explanation: 此為動詞，表「放置」。  同 put

examples.en: As soon as David placed his backpack on the couch, he rushed out of the room.
examples.ch: David一把背包放在沙發上，他就衝出房間了。

grammar.explanation: 表「象徵；代表」。  同 stand for, symbolize

examples.en: Red roses are often used to represent romance and love.
examples.ch: 紅玫瑰常被用來象徵浪漫和愛情。

examples.en: A group of activists attempted to call the public’s attention to issues related to gender equality by holding a parade.
examples.ch: 一群倡議人士藉由舉辦遊行，企圖引起大眾對性別平等相關議題的注意。

grammar.explanation: 為表主格的關係代名詞，其後所引導的形容詞子句用以修飾逗點之前的先行詞。而tagging不須限定，所以which前須加逗點，表非限定用法。=補述用法

examples.en: Molly gave me a pot of rosemary, which is one of my favorite herbs.
examples.ch: Molly給了我一盆迷迭香，這是我最喜歡的香草植物之一。

grammar.explanation: 為一轉承片語，表「此外」。英文中有許多轉承詞／片語，可作為句子間的連結，讓文句更加流暢。  同 besides, furthermore, moreover, what’s more

examples.en: Donald Trump signed an executive order in May 2020. In addition , he waged war against social media platforms like Twitter and Facebook.
examples.ch: 唐納•川普2020年5月時簽署了一項行政命令。此外，他還對推特和臉書等社群媒體平臺宣戰。
